steps.title: Substitute x = -10 into g(x) to find g(-10)
steps.content:
We need to work from the inside out. First, evaluate $g(-10)$ using the definition $g(x) = |13x - 9|$:

$$g(-10) = |13(-10) - 9|$$
$$= |-130 - 9|$$
$$= |-139|$$
$$= 139$$

steps.title: Substitute g(-10) into f(x) to find f(-10)
steps.content:
Now use the result from Step 1 to evaluate $f(-10)$ using the definition $f(x) = 5(g(x)) - 2$:

$$f(-10) = 5(g(-10)) - 2$$
$$= 5(139) - 2$$
$$= 695 - 2$$
$$= 693$$

That matches choice D — D is correct.
